Article Title: Watch: Abhijeet Dipke claims BJP workers assaulted reporters in front of Delhi Police
Article Text:
Abhijeet Dipke shared videos on X alleging that BJP workers assaulted reporters and protesters at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. (Screengrab/X/@abhijeet_dipke)

NEW DELHI: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke on Friday alleged that BJP workers physically assaulted reporters and protesters at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, sharing videos of the incident on X.In one of the videos, a voice can be heard saying, "our members are getting hit in front of Delhi police ." ChatGPT could not independently verify the claim or the circumstances shown in the footage.Sharing the video, Dipke wrote, "BJP goons physically assaulting reporters and people at Jantar Mantar."In another post, accompanied by a separate video, he wrote, "Authorities have a problem with pillows too now. Wish they were this efficient and proactive on paper leaks."The allegations come as Dipke's sit-in protest at Jantar Mantar demanding Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation entered its seventh day. The protest centres on alleged irregularities in competitive examinations and the NEET paper leak controversy.Earlier in the day, Dipke marked Pradhan's birthday by writing, "Happy Birthday, Pradhan. Please resign," on a whiteboard while supporters sang the birthday song.Addressing the minister in a video, he said, "A very happy birthday to you, Dharmendra Pradhan. Do us a favour and resign. As a birthday gift, we can even send you the resignation letter, you'll only have to put your thumb on it."Dipke has maintained that he will continue the protest until Pradhan resigns. He has also alleged that the Centre has not reached out for talks, claiming, "They are calling us terrorists instead."There was no immediate response from the BJP or Delhi Police to Dipke's allegations.
Article Summary: Abhijeet Dipke shared videos on X alleging that BJP workers assaulted reporters and protesters at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. In one of the videos, a voice can be heard saying, "our members are getting hit in front of Delhi police." ChatGPT could not independently verify the claim or the circumstances shown in the footage.

Article Title: 'Make sorry your middle name': BJP mocks Rahul Gandhi over expressing regret in defamation case by Shivraj Singh Chouhan's son
Article Text:
Shivraj Singh Chouhan; Rahul Gandhi

Also Read |

NEW DELHI: The BJP on Thursday said Congress MP Rahul Gandhi should make "Sorry" his middle name after he expressed regret over alleged defamatory remarks against Kartikeya Singh, son of Union minister and senior BJP leader Shivraj Singh Chouhan Ahead of the 2018 Madhya Pradesh assembly elections, Gandhi, then the Congress president, had linked Kartikeya Singh to the Panama Papers leak scandal during a public speech. The remarks prompted Kartikeya Singh to file a defamation case against the Congress leader in a Bhopal MP-MLA court.On Wednesday, Rahul Gandhi's lawyer informed the Madhya Pradesh high court that the statement was not related to Kartikeya Singh.Reacting to this, BJP spokesperson Amit Malviya wrote on X: "Rahul Gandhi should perhaps make 'Sorry' his middle name. One has lost count of the number of times he has had to apologise, express regret, or retract his defamatory and irresponsible statements. The latest instance comes in the defamation case filed by Kartikeya Singh, son of Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan. Rahul Gandhi has now approached the Madhya Pradesh high court expressing regret, stating that the remarks in question were not even related to Kartikeya Singh.Malviya also questioned why the Lok Sabha leader of the opposition made the statement if it was not about Kartikeya Singh."Time and again, Rahul Gandhi makes sensational allegations, grabs headlines, and when challenged in court or confronted with facts, retreats behind apologies and expressions of regret. A Leader of Opposition is expected to exercise responsibility, not indulge in reckless accusations and then disown them later. For Rahul Gandhi, defamation seems to have become a habit and apologies a recurring ritual. The country deserves better than a leader who speaks first and thinks later," he added.Rahul Gandhi has previously stated that he is not "not Savarkar to apologise" — a reference to right-wing ideologue Vinayak Damodar Savarkar, whom the Congress has accused of seeking clemency from the British during the freedom struggle.In 2019, Gandhi tendered an unconditional apology to the Supreme Court over remarks linked to its observations in the Rafale fighter jet case.At the time, Gandhi had repeatedly targeted Prime Minister Narendra Modi over the Rafale deal and popularised the slogan "chowkidar chor hai" (the watchman is a thief) during the Lok Sabha election campaign.The BJP subsequently moved a contempt petition against him, alleging that he had incorrectly attributed remarks to the apex court.
Article Summary: Congress MP Rahul Gandhi expressed regret over alleged defamatory remarks against Kartikeya Singh, son of Shivraj Singh Chouhan. Gandhi, then the Congress president, had linked Singh to the Panama Papers leak scandal during a public speech. The remarks prompted Singh to file a defamation case against the Congress leader in a Bhopal MP-MLA court. Rahul Gandhi's lawyer informed the Madhya Pradesh high court that the statement was not related to Singh.

Article Title: Amit Shah calls on president amid buzz of reshuffle of union council of ministers
Article Text:
Amit Shah calls on president amid buzz of reshuffle of union council of ministers

NeEW DELHI: Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Thursday called on President Droupadi Murmu at the Rashtrapati Bhavan here amid the buzz over a reshuffle of the union council of ministers.The president's office shared a picture of the meeting in a post on X."Union Minister for Home Affairs and Cooperation Shri Amit Shah called on President Droupadi Murmu at Rashtrapati Bhavan," the post said.On Tuesday, Prime Minister Narendra Modi had called on President Murmu.The meetings assume significance given the speculation that a reshuffle of the union council of ministers is imminent.Veteran BJP leader from Kerala George Kurian recently resigned from the council of ministers after his Rajya Sabha termed expired. The 65-year-old was Union minister of state for minority affairs and also for fisheries, animal husbandry and dairying.The BJP decided not to renominate Kurian, whose term in the Upper House ended on June 21.Besides, Minister of State for Railways Ravneet Singh, whose Rajya Sabha term also ended on June 21, has not been renominated by the BJP to the Upper House either.Singh, who had been a Rajya Sabha member from Rajasthan since August 2024, also holds charge as the minister of state for food processing industries.A few Union ministers have been given key organisational responsibilities in their home states. Their appointment fuelled the buzz of a possible reshuffle.BJP national president Nitin Nabin on May 28 appointed Union Minister of State for Road Transport and Highways and Corporate Affairs Harsh Malhotra as Delhi BJP chief. In December last year, Union Minister of State for Finance Pankaj Chaudhary was the new president of the BJP's Uttar Pradesh unit.
Article Summary: Amit Shah calls on president amid buzz of reshuffle of union council of ministers. Prime Minister Narendra Modi had called on President Droupadi Murmu on Tuesday. The meetings assume significance given the speculation that a reshuffle is imminent.Veteran BJP leader from Kerala George Kurian recently resigned from the council.

Article Title: 'Not related to him': Rahul Gandhi expresses regret in defamation case filed by Shivraj Singh Chouhan's son
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi (File Photo)

Poll Do you think alleged defamatory statement by Rahul Gandhi was warranted? Yes, it was justified. No, it was inappropriate.





BHOPAL: Congress MP and Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi has approached the Madhya Pradesh high court, expressing regret over his alleged defamatory statement involving union minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan ’s son Kartikeya Singh.The application was filed by Rahul's lawyer on Wednesday, stating that the remark made by the Congress leader was not related to Singh.Kartikeya Singh had filed a defamation complaint against the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha before a Bhopal court, alleging that Gandhi’s statement had harmed his reputation.In his complaint filed before the court handling cases against MPs and MLAs, Singh alleged that during an election rally in Jhabua in 2018, Gandhi had referred to his name while speaking about the Panama Papers leak scandal.Following the complaint, the court issued a summons directing Gandhi to appear personally. The Congress leader then moved the High Court seeking to quash the summons and the defamation proceedings.Justice Pramod Kumar Agarwal will hear Gandhi’s petition on Thursday.During Wednesday’s hearing, the petitioner produced records of the lower court as directed by the high court. Advocate Sankalp Kochhar appeared for the complainant before the high court.
Article Summary: Kartikeya Singh had filed a defamation complaint against the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha. Singh alleged that during an election rally in Jhabua in 2018, Gandhi had referred to his name while speaking about the Panama Papers leak scandal. Gandhi moved the High Court seeking to quash the summons and the defamation proceedings.

Article Title: State ordered to set up SIT probe in Pune realtor murder
Article Text:
Ketan Agarwal





Mumbai: The presiding officer in the state assembly, Raju Khare, directed the government to set up an SIT probe in the Ketan Agarwal murder case at Pune’s Lohagad on Thursday.“This is a serious issue. An SIT probe should be conducted,” said Khare. The demand for the probe was made by BJP MLA Sudhir Mungantiwar.The issue was raised in the assembly by NCP’s Sunil Shelke, who demanded action against the accused Siya Goyal’s family. “Siya Goyal was in relationship with her companion for many months but her family hid it from Ketan’s family. Action should be taken against them and there should be a trial in a fast-track court,” said Shelke.However, SS UBT MLA Bhaskar Jadhav sought clarification on whether a decision taken by the presiding officer has any bearing on the government. He pointed to the incident in March when deputy chairperson of the state council Neelam Gorhe had suspended the superintendent of police in Satara for allegedly manhandling cabinet minister Shamburaj Desai during the zilla parishad polls. Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis did not allow the suspension and said the legislature could not interfere in the functioning of the executive.
Article Summary: The demand for the probe was made by BJP MLA Sudhir Mungantiwar. The issue was raised in the assembly by NCP’s Sunil Shelke. “Siya Goyal was in relationship with her companion for many months but her family hid it from Ketan's family. Action should be taken against them and there should be a trial in a fast-track court,” said Shelke, an NCP member.

Article Title: Patil vs Rajenimbalkar: Dharashiv funding credit war heats up
Article Text:
Ranajagjitsinha Patil

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





A day after the state urban development department, led by deputy CM Eknath Shinde , sanctioned Rs 18 crore for the Dharashiv Municipal Council, MP Omprakash Rajenimbalkar, who recently switched to Shiv Sena, and Tuljapur BJP MLA Ranajagjitsinha Patil both claimed credit for securing the funds.The development comes amid the long-standing family rivalry between the two politicians, now extending into a second generation, and continuing despite both being part of Mahayuti.Rajenimbalkar said he had been consistently pursuing the demand for funds for Dharashiv’s development. He thanked chief minister Devendra Fadnavis, deputy CM Sunetra Pawar, and particularly deputy CM Shinde for initiating what he called a “new chapter” in the city’s growth.Patil described the sanction as the outcome of sustained follow-up efforts, saying, “Persistent efforts for the all-round development of Dharashiv city have yielded significant success, with the approval of Rs 18 crore under key state schemes. Mahayuti govt remains committed to the city’s progress and this fund will provide strong momentum for future development.”The rivalry between the two families dates back to 2006, when Rajenimbalkar’s father, Pawanraje, was murdered. Patil’s father, Padamsinh Patil, was named as an accused in the case but was recently acquitted by a special CBI court. Following Rajenimbalkar’s switch to Shinde’s faction on June 21, Patil had expressed hope that the long-standing feud would come to an end. Rajenimbalkar, however, made it clear that political alignment would not erase the rivalry. “Irrespective of party affiliations, I will not hesitate to oppose such mentalities,” he said.Responding to this, Patil declined to escalate the matter, stating he would comment at an appropriate time.
Article Summary: MP Omprakash Rajenimbalkar and Tuljapur BJP MLA Ranajagjitsinha Patil both claimed credit for securing the funds. The development comes amid the long- standing family rivalry between the two politicians, now extending into a second generation.

Article Title: Mushrif hints at larger share of seats in Mahayuti panel for Gokul dairy poll, says NCP always been elder brother in cooperatives
Article Text:
A poster put up by Satej Patil at Gokul Dairy’s administrative office in Tarabai Park highlights achievements from the past five years

Kolhapur

:





Medical education minister and senior NCP member Hasan Mushrif on Thursday claimed to have the support of 1,971 primary milk societies, each entitled to one vote, to elect directors of the Kolhapur Zilla Sahakari Dudh Utpadak Sangh, popularly known as Gokul Dairy.These societies have submitted resolutions authorising representatives, called ‘Tharav Dharak', to vote on their behalf. With 1,971 resolutions filed of around 4,500 eligible societies, Mushrif has demonstrated NCP’s dominant position within Mahayuti. “NCP has always been the elder brother in the cooperative sector. We will contest the poll together as Mahayuti, and seat-sharing will be finalised after the election authorities approve the voters’ list,” the minister said.Over the last few months, Mushrif has faced allegations of corruption and nepotism from BJP members, including Chandgad MLA Shivaji Patil and Mahadev Belawade, who has also approached courts, challenging the eligibility of over 1,300 societies. The matter is currently under judicial review.State govt has assured the Supreme Court that Gokul Dairy election will be conducted within 90 days. Following this, election authorities directed primary milk societies to submit resolutions authorising their voting representatives.Mushrif announced that these authorised voters would be sent on religious tours, a move aimed at preventing them from switching loyalties.During the previous election, Mushrif had contested alongside Congress MLC Satej Patil , members from the undivided Shiv Sena and the Jansurajya Shakti Party. This time, however, under the guidance of senior members, the decision has been taken to contest as part of Mahayuti. As a result, Patil has formed a separate panel, named ‘Shahu Samvichari Panel’.At Gokul Dairy’s administrative office in Tarabai Park, a poster put up by Satej Patil highlighted achievements during the last five years, including higher milk procurement prices benefiting farmers. Through this, Patil has appealed to the voters to support his panel to “save Gokul”.Mushrif, however, credited the dairy’s progress over the past five years to Mahayuti, pointing out that all recent chairpersons were affiliated with alliance parties — Vishwas Abaji Patil (Shiv Sena), Arun Dongale (NCP) and his son Naved Mushrif (NCP). “Under their leadership, Gokul Dairy expanded operations, set up a solar plant in Solapur, upgraded facilities in Pune and increased procurement prices, thereby providing financial relief to farmers,” Mushrif added.
Article Summary: Hasan Mushrif claims to have support of 1,971 primary milk societies, each entitled to one vote. These societies have submitted resolutions authorising representatives, called ‘Tharav Dharak', to vote on their behalf.

Article Title: Teen who rammed SUV into woman before crashing into 6 vehicles in Navi Mumbai booked for drunk driving
Article Text:
Drunk teen SUV driver drags woman 50m, hits 6 parked vehicles

Watch CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash

NAVI MUMBAI: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver.The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside in Artist village, sector-8 on Friday.Arun Pawar, senior inspector of CBD-Belapur said, the accused SUV driver Vikram Kamble's medical test report received from NMMC hospital, Nerul confirmed that he was under the influence of alcohol while driving.Subsequently, relevant section 185 Of Motor Vehicle Act that penalizes drunk driving were invoked against him and will be mentioned in the chargesheet that will be submitted in the court.The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees is recuperating at MGM hospital, CBD-Belapur and is out of danger, informed doctors.The accident took place in the residential locality of Artist village in Sector-8, CBD-Belapur around 5.30 pm on Friday. Police said the speeding SUV hit the woman from behind while she was walking close to the pavement and dragged her for nearly 50 metres before crashing into six vehicles parked along the roadside.Local residents rushed the injured woman to MGM Hospital, where she received prompt treatment. The incident was also captured on CCTV cameras installed in the area.Police said the SUV driver Vikram Gangadhar Kamble (19) was accompanied by his relative Akash Chandrakant Kamble (26), a private chauffeur. Both were detained by locals at the spot before the police arrived, following which an FIR was registered for rash and negligent driving and damage to property.
Article Summary: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver. The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside. The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees.

Article Title: 16-year-old girl gang-raped by two men aged over 60 near Mumbai
Article Text:
(The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on cases related to sexual assault)





THANE: A 16-year-old girl was allegedly raped by two senior citizens in Maharashtra's Thane district, police said on Sunday.The accused, both above 60 years of age, were arrested in connection with the attack, which occurred in a slum colony in Netivali village on Saturday night.According to police, the duo allegedly caught hold of the girl and raped her repeatedly.The survivor managed to escape their clutches and lodged a complaint with the police.The accused have been booked under the relevant provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act.
Article Summary: A 16-year-old girl was allegedly raped by two senior citizens in Maharashtra's Thane district. The accused, both above 60 years of age, were arrested in connection with the attack. The survivor managed to escape their clutches and lodged a complaint with the police.

Article Title: Supriya targets BJP over waterlogging in Mumbai & Pune
Article Text:
Heavy rainfall on Wednesday led to widespread waterlogging and massive traffic congestion across the city. Severe water accumulation at Tathawade, disrupted vehicular movement and caused delays

Pune:





NCP (SP) working president Supriya Sule on Thursday said that instead of focusing on governance, the party governing PMC and BMC is busy breaking up other parties and spending on advertisements. She was criticising the BJP over waterlogging in Pune and Mumbai after the first monsoon rain this week.Supriya said, “When the BJP was in the opposition, it would criticise NCP for Pune’s infrastructural issues and Shiv Sena for Mumbai’s issues. However, now that it is governing both PMC and BMC, the situation has worsened in both cities. When they (BJP) are governing both municipal corporations, they should focus on improving infrastructure but they are busy breaking up parties and families and spending on advertisements,” she added.Refuting speculation of a split in the opposition after some MLAs skipped a meeting called in Mumbai on Wednesday, Supriya said, “Our party’s MLAs informed us about their prior engagements. Similarly, Congress and Shiv Sena (UBT) leaders would know about their MLAs. MLAs from both sides have skipped assembly sessions.”After six of its MPs joined the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, Shiv Sena (UBT) urged Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla not to accord separate recognition to the rebels. Reacting to this demand, Supriya said, “Our party has gone through the same experience. Sharad Pawar is the founder of the NCP but was removed from the post of party chief. I do not have much hope that the decision will go in favour of Shiv Sena (UBT).”
Article Summary: NCP (SP) working president Supriya Sule was criticising the BJP over waterlogging in Pune and Mumbai after the first monsoon rain this week. She said instead of focusing on governance, the party governing PMC and BMC is busy breaking up other parties and spending on advertisements.

Article Title: Nashik MLC polls winner Gokul Gite joins Shiv Sena as associate member
Article Text:
Gite secured 357 votes in the election, defeating Darade by 109 votes and comfortably crossing the winning quota of 303.

Nashik

:

TOI





Hours after defeating Shiv Sena ’s official candidate Narendra Darade in the Nashik local authorities election, independent candidate Gokul Gite joined Shiv Sena as an associate member in the legislative council.The swift political move raised eyebrows among Shiv Sena and BJP politicians, while Darade claimed that deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde was well aware of how the party’s candidate was treated by BJP during the contest.“Even before the election results were officially declared, a close associate of Gokul Gite received a call from a Shiv Sena minister asking him to come to Mumbai by a chartered flight,” said a Shiv Sena member backing Darade. “Though the minister initially denied it, claiming such a quick move was impossible, he eventually flew to Nashik himself to bring Gite to Mumbai by plane. It strongly suggests that the entire sequence of events was pre-planned,” he added.A BJP politician also expressed surprise at the development. “On June 9, three ministers — Girish Mahajan, Uday Samant and Dada Bhuse — were persuading Gite to pull out of the race.Suddenly, after the results are out, MLC Gokul Gite, a close confidante of Girish Mahajan, is in Shiv Sena. This surprises everybody,” he said.Gite secured 357 votes in the election, defeating Darade by 109 votes and comfortably crossing the winning quota of 303.Darade, however, appeared to play it safe when asked if he shared these suspicions. “We were ready to contest the election straight forward manner, but were told it would go against the interests of Mahayuti. We were betrayed, and Shiv Sena chief and deputy CM Eknath Shinde is well aware of the situation, as well as how our alliance partner (BJP) treated Shiv Sena during the election,” he toldOn Gite’s sudden induction, Darade said he was informed by Shinde himself. “I was in Mumbai around 4pm on Monday when I received a call from deputy CM Shinde. He explained that it was crucial to strengthen the party’s presence in the legislative council and expand our footprint in Nashik district, which is why Gokul Gite was being brought in. I told him I had no objections,” he said.
Article Summary: Independent candidate Gokul Gite defeated Shiv Sena’s official candidate Narendra Darade in the Nashik local authorities election. Gite secured 357 votes in the election, defeating Darade by 109 votes and comfortably crossing the winning quota of 303.

Article Title: Death toll in Poonamallee accident rises to 2
Article Text:
Road tragedy

Chennai:





The death toll in the car accident near Poonamallee rose to two after a 27-year-old police constable succumbed to injuries at a government hospital on Thursday.The deceased, Arur Kumar ,27, along with Tamil Selvan ,32, and Saravanakumar ,28, all natives of Melur in Madurai district, were serving in the Armed Reserve Police in Chennai.The three policemen, along with two others, had travelled to Ranipet to attend a friend’s wedding four days ago. While returning to Chennai late at night, they stopped their car near Pazhanjur in Sembarambakkam, close to Poonamallee and some of them got down.At that time, a speeding car coming from behind rammed into the policemen standing on the roadside. Tamil Selvan died on the spot, while Arur Kumar and Saravanakumar sustained grievous injuries. Both injured policemen were admitted to the Government Kilpauk Medical College Hospital, where they were undergoing treatment. However, Arur Kumar succumbed to his injuries, taking the death toll in the accident to two.Avadi Traffic Investigation Wing police have registered a case and are investigating the circumstances surrounding the accident.
Article Summary: The three policemen, along with two others, had travelled to Ranipet to attend a friend’s wedding four days ago. While returning to Chennai late at night, they stopped their car near Pazhanjur in Sembarambakkam, close to Poonamallee and some of them got down. A speeding car coming from behind rammed into the policemen standing on the roadside.

Article Title: Nirmala Sitharaman welcomes TVK’s stand on administrative cooperation with Centre
Article Text:
File photo

Chennai:





Union finance minister Nirmala Sitharaman on Thursday welcomed chief minister C Joseph Vijay’s statement that Tamil Nadu govt would cooperate with Union govt on administrative matters though BJP is TVK’s ideological enemy.“As a Union minister, I welcome the announcement of TVK govt that it will cooperate with Union govt,’’ Nirmala told reporters in Kancheepuram after a book release event.“Our Prime Minister Narendra Modi has never asked me to differentiate between states on fund allocation and distribution of projects. Tamil Nadu received its due share of funds. We implemented some mega projects in Tuticorin. Union govt will still extend the same cooperation to Tamil Nadu and disburse all funds,” Nirmala said.On Tamil Nadu’s rising debt, she said there is nothing wrong in states borrowing funds, but it should be used for capital investments. “When we borrow, we should create assets such as schools, hospitals or industries, which can cater to the needs of people for the next 50 years,” she said.“If that was done, economy will also grow. But what DMK did was revenue expenditure where they gave money to the people and offered salary hike. I am not saying that you shouldn’t do this, but do this when you have excess revenue,” she added.She justified Vijay’s ‘short story’ taunting former CM M K Stalin for his defeat in the recently assembly election. “There is nothing wrong in it. Where else will you do politics if not for assembly,” the minister asked.
Article Summary: Nirmala Sitharaman welcomed chief minister C Joseph Vijay’s statement that Tamil Nadu govt would cooperate with Union govt on administrative matters. “Our Prime Minister Narendra Modi has never asked me to differentiate between states on fund allocation and distribution of projects,’’ she said.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Kanyakumari man duped of Rs11.12 lakh in fake dealership scam; 2 held from West Bengal
Article Text:
A 28-year-old man from Kanyakumari district was cheated of Rs11.12 lakh by fraudsters who promised him an electric bike dealership through a fake website.

Kanyakumari

:





A 28-year-old man from Kanyakumari district was cheated of Rs11.12 lakh by fraudsters who promised him an electric bike dealership through a fake website.Police said Sundar , from Aralvaimozhi, had applied for a dealership on a website resembling an official portal. Soon after, unidentified people contacted him via WhatsApp , assuring him of securing the dealership in Kanyakumari district. Citing various charges, including registration fees, no-objection certificate (NOC) charges, and license fees, they persuaded him to transfer money in multiple installments.Realising he had been duped and no one had contacted him thereafter, Sundar lodged a complaint with the cybercrime police.Acting on the complaint, a case was registered under the orders of Kanyakumari district superintendent of police Dr R Stalin. A special team led by Inspector Parvathi traced the money trail and bank account details.Based on the investigation, a team headed by sub-inspector Ajmal Jenif travelled to West Bengal and arrested Banti Mondal, 28, and Ujjal Karmakar, 30, on a transit warrant. They were brought to Nagercoil on Tuesday for further investigation.The accused were produced before court and remanded in judicial custody the same day.Further investigation is under way to identify other members of the racket.
Article Summary: Police said Sundar, from Aralvaimozhi, had applied for a dealership on a website resembling an official portal. Unidentified people contacted him via WhatsApp assuring him of securing the dealership in Kanyakumari district. They persuaded him to transfer money in multiple installments.

Article Title: Madurai youth’s organs save four lives
Article Text:
Madurai

:





The organs of a 28-year-old youth who died following a road accident were donated, helping save the lives of four people.Muralikannan from Madurai district was riding a two-wheeler when he rammed into the Palanganatham bridge wall around 5.10pm on Monday. He suffered a severe head injury and was admitted to the Government Rajaji Hospital (GRH), Madurai, in an unconscious state. Despite treatment by the neurosurgery and intensive care teams, Muralikannan was declared brain dead at 5.15am on Wednesday. His parents consented to organ donation , following which the harvesting procedure was carried out at the trauma care centre.His liver was donated to Apollo Hospital, Madurai, while one kidney was transplanted at Government Mahatma Gandhi Memorial Hospital and the other at Dr Murugan Hospital in Trichy. His corneas were donated to GRH, Madurai.After postmortem examination at GRH, the body was handed over to the relatives through the police department. Hospital authorities paid homage to the deceased, while the Madurai district administration accorded state honours.
Article Summary: Muralikannan from Madurai district was riding a two-wheeler when he rammed into the Palanganatham bridge wall around 5.10pm on Monday. He suffered a severe head injury and was admitted to the Government Rajaji Hospital (GRH), Madurai, in an unconscious state. Despite treatment by the neurosurgery and intensive care teams, he was declared brain dead at 5.15am on Wednesday.
